---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Clayton Adamson"
lang: en
output:
  html_document:
    css: styles.css
    theme: paper
    includes:
      in_header: header.html
---



<!-- =============================================================================
GLOBAL WRAPPER (stabilizes float layout)
============================================================================= -->
<div id="page-wrapper">

<!-- =============================================================================
CUSTOM CSS FOR 3-COLUMN LAYOUT
============================================================================= -->
<style>
.column-left{
  float: left;
  width: 30%;
  text-align: left;
}
.column-center{
  display: inline-block;
  width: 4%;
  text-align: left;
}
.column-right{
  float: right;
  width: 66%;
  text-align: left;
}
</style>

<br>

<!-- =============================================================================
LEFT COLUMN
============================================================================= -->
<div class="column-left">

<img src="images/headshot_website.JPG" style="width: 95%" alt="Clayton Adamson headshot">

<p style="font-family: 'DM Mono'; font-size: 16pt; color: Black; letter-spacing: 1.5px; line-height: 2.5; margin: 0; padding: 1;">
<strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>
</p>

<div style="font-size: 2pt;"><br></div>

<p style="font-family: 'DM Mono'; font-size: 12pt; color: Black; letter-spacing: 1px; line-height: 2; margin: 0; padding: 1;">
PhD Candidate in Sociology
</p>

<div style="font-size: 2pt;"><br></div>

<p style="font-family: 'DM Mono'; font-size: 12pt; color: Black; letter-spacing: 1px; line-height: 2; margin: 0; padding: 1;">
Center for Demography and Ecology
</p>

<p style="font-family: 'DM Mono'; font-size: 12pt; color: Black; letter-spacing: 1px; border-bottom: .5pt solid; line-height: 2; margin: 0; padding: .5pt;"></p>

<div style="font-size: 4pt;"><br></div>

<p style="font-family: 'DM Mono'; font-size: 12pt; color: Black; letter-spacing: 2px;">
University of Wisconsin—Madison
</p>

<div style="font-size: 4pt;"><br></div>

<p style="font-family: 'DM Mono'; font-size: 12pt; letter-spacing: 1px; color: #3C2F2F;">
cadamson *[at]* wisc *[dot]* edu
</p>

<div style="font-size: 10pt;"><br></div>

<h2 style="border-bottom: .5pt solid; line-height: 2;"> Links</h2>

<p>
<a href="CV_Adamson_26.06.02.pdf" style="font-size: 14pt; font-family: 'DM Mono'; letter-spacing: 1px; color: #0B5394;"><strong>Curriculum Vitae</strong></a>
</p>

<p>
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=4DMBuDkAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=sra"
style="font-size: 14pt; font-family: 'DM Mono'; letter-spacing: 1px; color: #1E6649;">
<strong>Google Scholar</strong>
</a>
</p>

</div>

<!-- =============================================================================
CENTER SPACER
============================================================================= -->
<div class="column-center"></div>

<!-- =============================================================================
RIGHT COLUMN
============================================================================= -->
<div class="column-right">

<h1>About</h1>

<p style="font-size: 10pt; letter-spacing: 1px; border-bottom: 1.5pt solid; line-height: 2; margin: 0; padding: .5pt;"></p>

<div style="font-size: 4pt;"><br></div>

<div style="line-height: 1.4;">

<p style="text-align: justify;">
My name is Clayton Adamson and I am a PhD Candidate in Sociology at the University of Wisconsin–Madison. Previously, I obtained a M.S. in Regional Science from Cornell University. My research interests include place, social stratification, demography, economic development, and family sociology.
</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">
My research agenda follows two themes:
</p>

<ol style="margin-left: 1.2em; text-align: justify;">
  <li>
    <strong>How place and geography shape stratification within the US.</strong>
    This first stream includes work on how emplaced economic structures limit resident prosperity, how geographic origins shape returns to migration, and how broad temporal shifts in the American family vary spatially.
  </li>

  <li>
    <strong>How noneconomic family characteristics shape intergenerational persistence.</strong>
    This second stream investigates how parental attributes such as occupational job quality and alcohol abuse intersect with and transcend traditional determinants of child status attainment.
  </li>
</ol>

<p style="text-align: justify;">
I regularly use various advanced methodological approaches, including: spatial econometrics, machine learning, Bayesian approaches, network science, and non-linear spline and growth models. The majority of my work relies upon U.S. public survey and administrative data and longitudinal data from the Panel Study of Income Dynamics (PSID), along with equivalent data in the UK. My research has been published in journals such as <strong><em>Social Science Research</em>, <em>Social Networks</em>, and <em>Spatial Demography</em></strong>.
</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">
Prior to academia, I worked as a director of economic modeling and research manager in economic development organizations and think-tank settings. Beyond research, I spend my time running, hiking, and cycling, reading classic literature, listening to blues-rock and alt-country music, and rooting for the Brewers, Packers, and Badgers.
</p>

</div>

<div style="font-size: 1px;"><br></div>

<h1>Research</h1>

<p style="font-size: 12pt; letter-spacing: 1px; border-bottom: 1.5pt solid; line-height: 1; margin: 0; padding: .5pt;"></p>

<div style="height: 6px;"></div>

<h2>● Published & Forthcoming Papers</h2>

<p style="font-size: 11pt; letter-spacing: 1px; border-bottom: .5pt solid; color: #3C2F2F; line-height: 2; margin: 0; padding: .5pt;"></p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h3>2026</h3>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>The Intergenerational Mobility Advantage of Educators’ Children: Capital Embedded in Occupations</h4>
<p><strong>Clayton Adamson</strong> and Karl Vachuska.<br>
<em>Social Science Research</em></p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>Neighborhood Shocks and Network Dynamics: An Instrumental Variable Approach to Measuring Triadic Closure in Daily Mobility Networks</h4>
<p>Karl Vachuska and <strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>.<br>
<em>Social Networks</em></p>

<div style="font-size: 2pt;"><br></div>

<h3>2025</h3>

<div style="height: 2px;"></div>

<h4><a href="https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12068858/">Economic Complexity and Divergent Population Growth by Race and Rurality</a></h4>
<p><strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>, Katherine J. Curtis, and Sara Peters.<br>
<em>Spatial Demography</em></p>

<div style="height: 6px;"></div>

<h2>○ Under Review</h2>

<p style="font-size: 12pt; letter-spacing: 1px; border-bottom: .5pt solid; color: #3C2F2F; line-height: 2; margin: 0; padding: .5pt;"></p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>Uneven Jobs, Uneven Places: Industrial Structure and the Geography of Job Quality</h4>
<p><strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>.<br> 
Revise and resubmit at <em>Population Research and Policy Review</em>.</p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>Growth Without Gain: The Adverse Effects of State and Local Development Incentives on Resident Prosperity</h4>
<p><strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>. <br>
Revise and resubmit at <em>Urban Affairs Review</em>.</p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>Unequal Returns to Growth and Decline Across U.S. Regions and Social Classes</h4>
<p><strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>. <br>
Revise and resubmit at <em>Cambridge Journal of Regions, Economy, and Society</em>.</p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>Housing Costs and US Fertility: A New Era</h4>
<p>Nicholas D.E. Mark, <strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>, and Max Besbris. <br>
Under review.</p>

<div style="height: 4px;"></div>

<h4>Neighborhoods, Homicide, and Racial Inequality in Life Expectancy: The Impact of Residence and Routine Mobility</h4>
<p>Karl Vachuska, <strong>Clayton Adamson</strong>, Brian Levy, and Max Besbris. <br>
Under review.</p>

<br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br>

</div>

<div style="clear: both;"></div>

<footer class="full-width-footer">
  <p>&copy; `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")` Clayton Adamson. All rights reserved.</p>
</footer>

</div>
